output.summary: Replace description RichText and buttons attributes with InnerBlocks in ContentWithMedia block. ThemeHeading will only handle eyebrow and heading. InnerBlocks will allow core/paragraph, core/list, core/quote, takt/button-row (and other text content blocks) but NOT headings. Default template: one core/paragraph and one takt/button-row with a primary button. Remove description and buttons attributes from block.json.

fileChanges.description: Remove description and buttons attributes; add innerBlocks to example
fileChanges.details: 1. Remove the 'description' attribute (line 39-41). 2. Remove the 'buttons' attribute (line 42-44). 3. Update the 'example' section to remove 'description' and 'buttons' from attributes and add an 'innerBlocks' array with a core/paragraph and a takt/button-row containing a takt/button child (following Text block.json pattern).

fileChanges.description: Replace ThemeHeading description/buttons with InnerBlocks; use ThemeHeading for eyebrow+heading only
fileChanges.details: 1. Add useInnerBlocksProps import from @wordpress/block-editor. 2. Remove 'description' and 'buttons' from ContentWithMediaAttributes type. 3. Add ALLOWED_BLOCKS constant: ['core/paragraph', 'core/list', 'core/quote', 'core/separator', 'takt/button-row'] (NO core/heading since ThemeHeading handles headings). 4. Add TEMPLATE constant: [['core/paragraph', {placeholder: 'Add your content here...'}], ['takt/button-row', {}, [['takt/button', {button: {url: '', variation: 'primary', title: ''}}]]]]. 5. Create innerBlocksProps via useInnerBlocksProps({className: 'content-with-media-inner-content'}, {allowedBlocks: ALLOWED_BLOCKS, template: TEMPLATE, templateLock: false}). 6. Modify ThemeHeading usage: pass enableDescription={false} and enableButtons={false} to disable description/buttons in ThemeHeading. Remove description and buttons props. 7. Render the InnerBlocks content area below the ThemeHeading inside the same content column div, using <div {...innerBlocksProps} />. 8. Remove description and buttons from the onChange handler and mergeAttributes logic.

fileChanges.description: Replace ThemeHeading description/buttons with $children InnerBlocks output
fileChanges.details:
1. Remove @var annotations for $description and $buttons. 2. Add @var string $children annotation. 3. In the ThemeHeading get_template_part call, remove 'description' and 'buttons' keys, and add 'enableDescription' => false and 'enableButtons' => false. 4. After the ThemeHeading get_template_part call (but still inside the same content column div), render the InnerBlocks content: if (!empty($children)), output
. 5. Remove $description and $buttons from the $hasContent-like checks if any exist (currently there are none, the block relies on ThemeHeading's own empty-check).

fileChanges.description: Update block registration if variations reference description/buttons in example attributes
fileChanges.details: Current variations only set mediaType attribute, so no changes needed to variation definitions. However, the save function import should be verified - since this block now has InnerBlocks, it needs InnerBlocks.Content in save or null save (dynamic block). The current save from @taktdev/utilities should handle this correctly as the block is dynamic (server-side rendered). No changes expected.

attributeChanges.name: description
attributeChanges.description: Removed because body text content will now be handled by InnerBlocks (core/paragraph, core/list, etc.) instead of a single RichText field.

attributeChanges.name: buttons
attributeChanges.description: Removed because buttons will now be handled by InnerBlocks using the takt/button-row block instead of ThemeHeading's built-in button array attribute.

designDecisions.question: Which blocks should be allowed in InnerBlocks?
designDecisions.recommendation: Allow core/paragraph, core/list, core/quote, core/separator, and takt/button-row. Exclude core/heading since ThemeHeading already handles the heading. This matches the user's request to allow text content blocks and the custom buttons block, but not headings.
designDecisions.evidence: User specified: 'InnerBlocks should accept: the custom buttons block, core/paragraph, core/list, and other text content blocks - but NOT headings'

designDecisions.question: What should the default InnerBlocks template contain?
designDecisions.recommendation: Template: [['core/paragraph', {placeholder: 'Add your content here...'}], ['takt/button-row', {}, [['takt/button', {button: {url: '', variation: 'primary', title: ''}}]]]]. This provides a paragraph for body text and a button row with a single primary button as the default starting content.
designDecisions.evidence: User specified: 'The default InnerBlocks template should be a core/paragraph and a button row with a primary button'

designDecisions.question: Where should InnerBlocks content render relative to the ThemeHeading?
designDecisions.recommendation: InnerBlocks content renders directly below the ThemeHeading inside the same content column div (the flex items-center wrapper). ThemeHeading handles eyebrow+heading only (with enableDescription=false, enableButtons=false), then InnerBlocks renders below it. The overall wrapper may need to change from 'flex items-center' to 'flex flex-col justify-center' to stack the ThemeHeading and InnerBlocks content vertically while maintaining vertical centering against the media column.
designDecisions.evidence: The Text block (blocks/Text/Text.tsx) uses ThemeHeading + InnerBlocks in a similar two-column layout, but in separate grid columns. For ContentWithMedia, both ThemeHeading and InnerBlocks go in the same content column, stacked vertically.

designDecisions.question: Should templateLock be set to false or left unlocked?
designDecisions.recommendation: Set templateLock to false (explicitly unlocked). This allows editors to add, remove, and reorder inner blocks freely. The ALLOWED_BLOCKS list already restricts which block types can be inserted.
designDecisions.evidence: The Text block uses templateLock={false}, and AccordionItem uses no templateLock. The pattern is consistent across the codebase.

designDecisions.question: Should the content wrapper use the 'discourse' class for InnerBlocks content?
designDecisions.recommendation: No. The 'discourse' class is used in blocks like Text and TabsItem where InnerBlocks content contains headings and needs full prose styling. For ContentWithMedia, the InnerBlocks content is paragraphs + buttons - not full prose content. Use a simple wrapper class 'content-with-media-inner-content' without discourse styling, since the paragraphs will use their default styling and the button-row already handles its own layout.
designDecisions.evidence: Text block uses 'discourse' wrapper because it supports core/heading and full rich text. ContentWithMedia InnerBlocks excludes headings and is simpler body text + buttons.

designDecisions.question: What spacing should exist between ThemeHeading (eyebrow+heading) and the InnerBlocks content area?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use gap-8 (32px) between ThemeHeading and InnerBlocks content, matching the existing spacing that ThemeHeading internally uses between its heading group and description group (the 'not-group-last:mb-8' class). The content wrapper div should use 'flex flex-col gap-8' or equivalent.
designDecisions.evidence: ThemeHeading.php uses 'not-group-last:mb-8' for spacing between heading group and description group. This represents the design intent for spacing between heading and body content.

designDecisions.question: How should the block handle migration from existing description/buttons attributes to InnerBlocks?
designDecisions.recommendation: This is a BREAKING CHANGE for existing content. Any existing ContentWithMedia blocks that have description or buttons attributes will lose that content when InnerBlocks is introduced, because the attributes are being removed and no deprecated/migrate handler is being added. If there are existing pages using this block with description/buttons content, a migration strategy is needed.
designDecisions.evidence: WordPress block deprecation API allows defining old attribute shapes and migrate functions. However, migrating from attributes to InnerBlocks requires a deprecated entry with migrate() that converts the old attributes to InnerBlocks. This is complex but important for data preservation.

innerBlocksPattern.description: Text block uses ThemeHeading + InnerBlocks in same component. ThemeHeading for eyebrow/heading/description/buttons, InnerBlocks for rich text content. Uses <InnerBlocks> component with allowedBlocks and template props.

useInnerBlocksPropsPattern.description: AccordionItem uses useInnerBlocksProps for InnerBlocks with allowedBlocks: core/paragraph, core/list, takt/button. Uses template: [['core/paragraph']].

buttonRowBlock.name: takt/button-row
buttonRowBlock.description: Button row container block. Contains takt/button children. The block name is takt/button-row (NOT takt/buttons). takt/button is a child block with parent: ['takt/button-row'].

phpChildrenPattern.description: In PHP, InnerBlocks content is available as $children. Render with: if (!empty($children)) { echo $children; }

blockJsonChanges.exampleChanges: Remove description and buttons from example.attributes. Add example.innerBlocks array with core/paragraph and takt/button-row + takt/button child.

phpChanges.themeHeadingChanges: Remove 'description' and 'buttons' keys. Add 'enableDescription' => false, 'enableButtons' => false.

qaChecklist: Verify InnerBlocks renders correctly in the editor below the eyebrow/heading
Verify core/paragraph can be added and edited
Verify core/list can be added and edited
Verify takt/button-row can be added with takt/button children
Verify core/heading CANNOT be added (restricted by allowedBlocks)
Verify default template loads with paragraph + button row on new block insertion
Verify frontend PHP renders $children content correctly
Verify dark mode styling applies correctly to InnerBlocks content
Verify isReversed layout works with InnerBlocks
Verify reverseRows (mobile ordering) works with InnerBlocks
Verify media column (image/video) still renders correctly alongside InnerBlocks content
Verify empty InnerBlocks state is handled gracefully
Verify block preview in inserter still works with updated example

questionsForUser.question: Should the InnerBlocks content area use the 'discourse' class for prose styling (typography reset for paragraphs, lists, etc.), or use a simpler wrapper without prose styling?
options: Use 'discourse' class for consistent prose styling
Use simple wrapper class without prose styling
questionsForUser.recommendation: Use simple wrapper without 'discourse' since the content is limited to paragraphs and buttons, not full article-like content. But if you want lists and quotes to have styled typography, 'discourse' may be appropriate.
questionsForUser.impact: minor - affects text styling of paragraphs and lists within the InnerBlocks area

questionsForUser.question: Should a block deprecation/migration handler be added to preserve existing description and buttons content from previously saved ContentWithMedia blocks? Without migration, any existing blocks using those attributes will lose their description and button data.
options: Add deprecated entry with migrate function to convert old description/buttons attributes to InnerBlocks
No migration needed - existing content can be manually re-entered
Keep description and buttons as hidden/legacy attributes that still render but are not editable (soft deprecation)
questionsForUser.recommendation: Add a deprecated entry with migrate() function to convert existing description text to a core/paragraph InnerBlock and existing buttons to a takt/button-row with takt/button children. This preserves existing content automatically.
questionsForUser.impact: critical - without migration, existing pages using ContentWithMedia will lose description and button content
